name: Invoice Ninja
short_desc: Self-hosted invoicing, expense tracking, and time billing for freelancers and small businesses.
author: Invoice Ninja
description: Invoice Ninja is an open-source invoicing platform for freelancers, agencies, and small businesses. Send branded invoices and quotes, track expenses, log billable time, accept online payments via 50+ gateways (Stripe, PayPal, Square, etc.), and run client portals — all from a self-hosted instance you control. This package bundles the Invoice Ninja v5 application server, an nginx reverse proxy, MariaDB 11, and a one-shot init container that prepares the data directories on first boot.

form_fields.label: Admin Email
form_fields.hint: Email used to create the initial Invoice Ninja administrator account on first boot.

form_fields.label: Admin Password
form_fields.hint: Password for the initial admin account. You can change it from the user settings page after install.

form_fields.label: MariaDB Root Password
form_fields.hint: Auto-generated MariaDB root password. The application connects via a separate `ninja` user (whose password is also `ninja`, scoped to localhost on the internal network).

form_fields.label: App Encryption Key
form_fields.hint: Auto-generated 32-byte key Invoice Ninja uses to encrypt session data and other Laravel secrets. Internally wrapped as `APP_KEY=base64:<this value>` for the container, so you don't need to run `artisan key:generate`. Don't rotate after first boot — existing encrypted data becomes unreadable.
